sections.heading: Child mortality rates fell nationally from 2022 to 2025
sections.body: Nationally, the five-year childhood mortality rates were lower in 2025 than in 2022. Under-5 mortality fell from 26 to 22 deaths per 1,000 live births, and infant mortality fell from 22 to 18. Neonatal mortality went down from 15 to 12, post-neonatal mortality from 7 to 6, and child mortality from 5 to 3.

sections.heading: Male children had higher mortality rates than female children in 2025
sections.body: In 2025, mortality rates were higher for male children than for female children on most measures. Under-5 mortality was 24 deaths per 1,000 live births for males, compared with 19 for females. Infant mortality was 19 for males and 17 for females, while neonatal mortality was 13 for males and 11 for females. Post-neonatal mortality was the same for both groups at 6. Child mortality was 5 for males and 2 for females.

sections.heading: Urban and rural under-5 mortality rates were almost the same in 2025
sections.body: Differences by residence were small in 2025. Under-5 mortality was 22 deaths per 1,000 live births in rural areas and 21 in urban areas. The smaller measures did not all point the same way: rural areas had lower neonatal and infant mortality than urban areas, while urban areas had lower post-neonatal and child mortality.

takeaways: National under-5 mortality fell from 26 deaths per 1,000 live births in 2022 to 22 in 2025.
In 2025, under-5 mortality was higher for male children than for female children, at 24 compared with 19 deaths per 1,000 live births.
Urban and rural under-5 mortality rates were nearly the same in 2025: 21 and 22 deaths per 1,000 live births.
